<!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--> [[Category:Geography of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Regions of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Spots of Okayama Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11577570}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11577570]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Yuga|Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada|{{ill|Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|ja|由加 (徳川宗尹側室)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|1=Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110899104}}}} '''Yugasan''' (由加山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山|1=mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8502}}, {{ill|shrine and temple|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine and temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}, and district located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in the Kojima region. The term can also refer collectively to all three as an area name. It is commonly called '''Yuga''' (由加). Historically, it was written as '''瑜伽山''', though the mountain's formal name remains "瑜伽山," while "由加山" is a colloquial usage.
The entire area is part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], within the Okayama region, designated as a {{ill|Second-Class Special Zone|ja|第二種特別地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second-Class Special Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and managed under the "Yugasan Park Area" conservation plan.<ref name="kokuritsukouen">[https://chushikoku.env.go.jp/nature/mat/data/m_1/okayama_03.pdf Conservation and Utilization Plan for the Okayama Region of Setonaikai National Park (PDF)] - Page 27</ref>
== Mountain == {{Infobox mountain | name = Yuga (瑜伽) Mountain | image = | elevation_m = 274 | coordinates = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan | range = Yuga Mountain Range | type = Hilly terrain | OSM_zoom = 10 }} Yugasan is a hilly area in northern Kojima, Kurashiki, with an elevation of 274 meters. Since ancient times, it has been a site of [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]] worship and [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]]. The official name is '''瑜伽山''' (Yugasan), and it is commonly listed as such on maps, though "由加山" is more frequently used in daily conversation.
Located in the central part of the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}}, the area is covered in natural forests and is part of Setonaikai National Park. In [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (733), the Buddhist monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] enshrined statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], naming them '''{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}'''. He also established a temple here. Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. It is renowned as the head temple of Yuga Daigongen and as a major {{ill|yakuyoke|ja|厄除け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuyoke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (calamity-warding) shrine, claiming a history of over 2,000 years.<ref name="kokuritsukouen"/>
In 733 (Tenpyō 5), under an imperial decree from [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], Gyōki founded the temple as Keibi-san Yuga-ji Maniju-in (now Rendaiji).
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the monk {{ill|Zōun|ja|増吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revived the temple. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. The Ikeda clan also built the shrine’s main hall and worship hall in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.
Originally a [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] site, it was separated under [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policies during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], with only the shrine section remaining as Yuga Shrine. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. As a result, Yuga Shrine took over the shrine facilities and started operating independently. Since then, both institutions have been completely separate, forbidding shared use of parking spaces and displaying signs emphasizing their separation. Legal disputes ensued, with the temple seeking to reclaim occupied structures.
Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. The port town of Tanokuchi and adjacent {{ill|Karagoto|ja|唐琴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karagoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimonochō|ja|下の町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonochō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also prospered. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. <!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--> [[Category:Geography of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Regions of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Spots of Okayama Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11577570}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11577570]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Yuga|Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada|{{ill|Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|ja|由加 (徳川宗尹側室)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|1=Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110899104}}}} '''Yugasan''' (由加山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山|1=mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8502}}, {{ill|shrine and temple|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine and temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}, and district located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in the Kojima region. The term can also refer collectively to all three as an area name. It is commonly called '''Yuga''' (由加). Historically, it was written as '''瑜伽山''', though the mountain's formal name remains "瑜伽山," while "由加山" is a colloquial usage.
The entire area is part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], within the Okayama region, designated as a {{ill|Second-Class Special Zone|ja|第二種特別地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second-Class Special Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and managed under the "Yugasan Park Area" conservation plan.<ref name="kokuritsukouen">[https://chushikoku.env.go.jp/nature/mat/data/m_1/okayama_03.pdf Conservation and Utilization Plan for the Okayama Region of Setonaikai National Park (PDF)] - Page 27</ref>
== Mountain == {{Infobox mountain | name = Yuga (瑜伽) Mountain | image = | elevation_m = 274 | coordinates = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan | range = Yuga Mountain Range | type = Hilly terrain | OSM_zoom = 10 }} Yugasan is a hilly area in northern Kojima, Kurashiki, with an elevation of 274 meters. Since ancient times, it has been a site of [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]] worship and [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]]. The official name is '''瑜伽山''' (Yugasan), and it is commonly listed as such on maps, though "由加山" is more frequently used in daily conversation.
Located in the central part of the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}}, the area is covered in natural forests and is part of Setonaikai National Park. In [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (733), the Buddhist monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] enshrined statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], naming them '''{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}'''. He also established a temple here. Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. It is renowned as the head temple of Yuga Daigongen and as a major {{ill|yakuyoke|ja|厄除け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuyoke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (calamity-warding) shrine, claiming a history of over 2,000 years.<ref name="kokuritsukouen"/>
In 733 (Tenpyō 5), under an imperial decree from [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], Gyōki founded the temple as Keibi-san Yuga-ji Maniju-in (now Rendaiji).
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the monk {{ill|Zōun|ja|増吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revived the temple. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. The Ikeda clan also built the shrine’s main hall and worship hall in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.
Originally a [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] site, it was separated under [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policies during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], with only the shrine section remaining as Yuga Shrine. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. As a result, Yuga Shrine took over the shrine facilities and started operating independently. Since then, both institutions have been completely separate, forbidding shared use of parking spaces and displaying signs emphasizing their separation. Legal disputes ensued, with the temple seeking to reclaim occupied structures.
Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. The port town of Tanokuchi and adjacent {{ill|Karagoto|ja|唐琴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karagoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimonochō|ja|下の町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonochō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also prospered. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. == Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.

The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

<!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--> [[Category:Geography of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Regions of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Spots of Okayama Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11577570}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11577570]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Yuga|Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada|{{ill|Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|ja|由加 (徳川宗尹側室)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|1=Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110899104}}}} '''Yugasan''' (由加山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山|1=mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8502}}, {{ill|shrine and temple|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine and temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}, and district located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in the Kojima region. The term can also refer collectively to all three as an area name. It is commonly called '''Yuga''' (由加). Historically, it was written as '''瑜伽山''', though the mountain's formal name remains "瑜伽山," while "由加山" is a colloquial usage.
The entire area is part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], within the Okayama region, designated as a {{ill|Second-Class Special Zone|ja|第二種特別地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second-Class Special Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and managed under the "Yugasan Park Area" conservation plan.<ref name="kokuritsukouen">[https://chushikoku.env.go.jp/nature/mat/data/m_1/okayama_03.pdf Conservation and Utilization Plan for the Okayama Region of Setonaikai National Park (PDF)] - Page 27</ref>
== Mountain == {{Infobox mountain | name = Yuga (瑜伽) Mountain | image = | elevation_m = 274 | coordinates = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan | range = Yuga Mountain Range | type = Hilly terrain | OSM_zoom = 10 }} Yugasan is a hilly area in northern Kojima, Kurashiki, with an elevation of 274 meters. Since ancient times, it has been a site of [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]] worship and [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]]. The official name is '''瑜伽山''' (Yugasan), and it is commonly listed as such on maps, though "由加山" is more frequently used in daily conversation.
Located in the central part of the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}}, the area is covered in natural forests and is part of Setonaikai National Park. In [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (733), the Buddhist monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] enshrined statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], naming them '''{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}'''. He also established a temple here. Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. It is renowned as the head temple of Yuga Daigongen and as a major {{ill|yakuyoke|ja|厄除け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuyoke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (calamity-warding) shrine, claiming a history of over 2,000 years.<ref name="kokuritsukouen"/>
In 733 (Tenpyō 5), under an imperial decree from [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], Gyōki founded the temple as Keibi-san Yuga-ji Maniju-in (now Rendaiji).
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the monk {{ill|Zōun|ja|増吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revived the temple. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. The Ikeda clan also built the shrine’s main hall and worship hall in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.
Originally a [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] site, it was separated under [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policies during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], with only the shrine section remaining as Yuga Shrine. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. As a result, Yuga Shrine took over the shrine facilities and started operating independently. Since then, both institutions have been completely separate, forbidding shared use of parking spaces and displaying signs emphasizing their separation. Legal disputes ensued, with the temple seeking to reclaim occupied structures.
Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. The port town of Tanokuchi and adjacent {{ill|Karagoto|ja|唐琴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karagoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimonochō|ja|下の町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonochō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also prospered. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

<!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--> [[Category:Geography of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Regions of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Spots of Okayama Prefecture]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11577570}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577570]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Yuga|Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada|{{ill|Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|ja|由加 (徳川宗尹側室)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|1=Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110899104}}}} '''Yugasan''' (由加山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山|1=mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8502}}, {{ill|shrine and temple|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine and temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}, and district located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in the Kojima region. The term can also refer collectively to all three as an area name. It is commonly called '''Yuga''' (由加). Historically, it was written as '''瑜伽山''', though the mountain's formal name remains "瑜伽山," while "由加山" is a colloquial usage.
The entire area is part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], within the Okayama region, designated as a {{ill|Second-Class Special Zone|ja|第二種特別地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second-Class Special Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and managed under the "Yugasan Park Area" conservation plan.<ref name="kokuritsukouen">[https://chushikoku.env.go.jp/nature/mat/data/m_1/okayama_03.pdf Conservation and Utilization Plan for the Okayama Region of Setonaikai National Park (PDF)] - Page 27</ref>
== Mountain == {{Infobox mountain | name = Yuga (瑜伽) Mountain | image = | elevation_m = 274 | coordinates = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan | range = Yuga Mountain Range | type = Hilly terrain | OSM_zoom = 10 }} Yugasan is a hilly area in northern Kojima, Kurashiki, with an elevation of 274 meters. Since ancient times, it has been a site of [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]] worship and [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]]. The official name is '''瑜伽山''' (Yugasan), and it is commonly listed as such on maps, though "由加山" is more frequently used in daily conversation.
Located in the central part of the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}}, the area is covered in natural forests and is part of Setonaikai National Park. In [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (733), the Buddhist monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] enshrined statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], naming them '''{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}'''. He also established a temple here. Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. It is renowned as the head temple of Yuga Daigongen and as a major {{ill|yakuyoke|ja|厄除け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuyoke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (calamity-warding) shrine, claiming a history of over 2,000 years.<ref name="kokuritsukouen"/>
In 733 (Tenpyō 5), under an imperial decree from [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], Gyōki founded the temple as Keibi-san Yuga-ji Maniju-in (now Rendaiji).
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the monk {{ill|Zōun|ja|増吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revived the temple. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. The Ikeda clan also built the shrine’s main hall and worship hall in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.
Originally a [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] site, it was separated under [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policies during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], with only the shrine section remaining as Yuga Shrine. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. As a result, Yuga Shrine took over the shrine facilities and started operating independently. Since then, both institutions have been completely separate, forbidding shared use of parking spaces and displaying signs emphasizing their separation. Legal disputes ensued, with the temple seeking to reclaim occupied structures.
Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. The port town of Tanokuchi and adjacent {{ill|Karagoto|ja|唐琴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karagoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimonochō|ja|下の町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonochō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also prospered. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. == Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

<!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577570]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Yuga|Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada|{{ill|Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|ja|由加 (徳川宗尹側室)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|1=Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110899104}}}} '''Yugasan''' (由加山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山|1=mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8502}}, {{ill|shrine and temple|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine and temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}, and district located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in the Kojima region. The term can also refer collectively to all three as an area name. It is commonly called '''Yuga''' (由加). Historically, it was written as '''瑜伽山''', though the mountain's formal name remains "瑜伽山," while "由加山" is a colloquial usage.
The entire area is part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], within the Okayama region, designated as a {{ill|Second-Class Special Zone|ja|第二種特別地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second-Class Special Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and managed under the "Yugasan Park Area" conservation plan.<ref name="kokuritsukouen">[https://chushikoku.env.go.jp/nature/mat/data/m_1/okayama_03.pdf Conservation and Utilization Plan for the Okayama Region of Setonaikai National Park (PDF)] - Page 27</ref>
== Mountain == {{Infobox mountain | name = Yuga (瑜伽) Mountain | image = | elevation_m = 274 | coordinates = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan | range = Yuga Mountain Range | type = Hilly terrain | OSM_zoom = 10 }} Yugasan is a hilly area in northern Kojima, Kurashiki, with an elevation of 274 meters. Since ancient times, it has been a site of [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]] worship and [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]]. The official name is '''瑜伽山''' (Yugasan), and it is commonly listed as such on maps, though "由加山" is more frequently used in daily conversation.
Located in the central part of the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}}, the area is covered in natural forests and is part of Setonaikai National Park. In [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (733), the Buddhist monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] enshrined statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], naming them '''{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}'''. He also established a temple here. Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. It is renowned as the head temple of Yuga Daigongen and as a major {{ill|yakuyoke|ja|厄除け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuyoke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (calamity-warding) shrine, claiming a history of over 2,000 years.<ref name="kokuritsukouen"/>
In 733 (Tenpyō 5), under an imperial decree from [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], Gyōki founded the temple as Keibi-san Yuga-ji Maniju-in (now Rendaiji).
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the monk {{ill|Zōun|ja|増吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revived the temple. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. The Ikeda clan also built the shrine’s main hall and worship hall in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.
Originally a [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] site, it was separated under [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policies during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], with only the shrine section remaining as Yuga Shrine. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. As a result, Yuga Shrine took over the shrine facilities and started operating independently. Since then, both institutions have been completely separate, forbidding shared use of parking spaces and displaying signs emphasizing their separation. Legal disputes ensued, with the temple seeking to reclaim occupied structures.
Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. The port town of Tanokuchi and adjacent {{ill|Karagoto|ja|唐琴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karagoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimonochō|ja|下の町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonochō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also prospered. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.

Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}

<!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--><!--Yuga Mountain--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->
{{wikidata link|Q11577570}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Geography of Kurashiki]] [[Category:Geography of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Mountains of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Natural Scenic Spots of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Regions of Okayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Sacred mountains of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yuga faith]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:由加山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577570]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{redirect|Yuga|Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada|{{ill|Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|ja|由加 (徳川宗尹側室)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|1=Yuga (Concubine of Tokugawa Munetada)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q110899104}}}} '''Yugasan''' (由加山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山岳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 山|1=mountain|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8502}}, {{ill|shrine and temple|ja|寺社|1=Temples and Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine and temple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temples and Shrines|qq=}}, and district located in [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], specifically in the Kojima region. The term can also refer collectively to all three as an area name. It is commonly called '''Yuga''' (由加). Historically, it was written as '''瑜伽山''', though the mountain's formal name remains "瑜伽山," while "由加山" is a colloquial usage.
The entire area is part of the [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]], within the Okayama region, designated as a {{ill|Second-Class Special Zone|ja|第二種特別地域|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second-Class Special Zone|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and managed under the "Yugasan Park Area" conservation plan.<ref name="kokuritsukouen">[https://chushikoku.env.go.jp/nature/mat/data/m_1/okayama_03.pdf Conservation and Utilization Plan for the Okayama Region of Setonaikai National Park (PDF)] - Page 27</ref>
== Mountain == {{Infobox mountain | name = Yuga (瑜伽) Mountain | image = | elevation_m = 274 | coordinates = | location = [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki, Okayama]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]], Japan | range = Yuga Mountain Range | type = Hilly terrain | OSM_zoom = 10 }} Yugasan is a hilly area in northern Kojima, Kurashiki, with an elevation of 274 meters. Since ancient times, it has been a site of [[:en:Iwakura rock|Iwakura (Shinto)]] worship and [[:en:Mountain worship|mountain worship]]. The official name is '''瑜伽山''' (Yugasan), and it is commonly listed as such on maps, though "由加山" is more frequently used in daily conversation.
Located in the central part of the {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}}, the area is covered in natural forests and is part of Setonaikai National Park. In [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]] (733), the Buddhist monk [[:en:Gyōki|Gyōki]] enshrined statues of [[:en:Amitābha|Amitābha]] and [[:en:Bhaisajyaguru|Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha]], naming them '''{{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}}'''. He also established a temple here. Yugasan is counted as one of {{ill|Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja|日本三大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan's Three Great Gongen Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
== Shrines and Temples == [[File:Brooklyn Museum - Yugasan in Bizan Province - Utagawa Hiroshige (Ando).jpg|thumb|280px|''Yugasan in Bizen Province'' by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] {{main|{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}|{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}}} Today, the area collectively known as "Yugasan" includes '''{{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}}''' and '''{{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}}'''. It is renowned as the head temple of Yuga Daigongen and as a major {{ill|yakuyoke|ja|厄除け|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yakuyoke|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 厄祓い|1=apotropaic magic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q620591}} (calamity-warding) shrine, claiming a history of over 2,000 years.<ref name="kokuritsukouen"/>
In 733 (Tenpyō 5), under an imperial decree from [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]], Gyōki founded the temple as Keibi-san Yuga-ji Maniju-in (now Rendaiji).
During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], the monk {{ill|Zōun|ja|増吽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zōun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} revived the temple. By the mid-[[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it became the prayer site for the ruling {{ill|Ikeda clan|ja|池田家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikeda clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 池田氏|1=Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975277}} of [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], with the domain lord visiting three times annually. The Ikeda clan also built the shrine’s main hall and worship hall in the [[:en:Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|Gongen-zukuri]] style.
Originally a [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] site, it was separated under [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]] policies during the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], with only the shrine section remaining as Yuga Shrine. Branch shrines were later built, leading to the name change to Yuga Shrine Hongū.
After [[:en:World War II|World War II]], the temple and shrine remained formally separate under different religious corporations, though they functioned as a single entity, with Buddhist monks conducting prayers in the main hall.
However, in March 1998, a conflict arose between the resident head monk and head priest, who were brothers. As a result, Yuga Shrine took over the shrine facilities and started operating independently. Since then, both institutions have been completely separate, forbidding shared use of parking spaces and displaying signs emphasizing their separation. Legal disputes ensued, with the temple seeking to reclaim occupied structures.
Despite this, visitors continue to refer to both as "Yugasan."
Historically, worshippers would visit both Yuga Shrine and [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] in [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]], a practice known as "{{ill|Ryōmairi|ja|両参り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryōmairi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." This custom is mentioned in the sequel to ''[[:en:Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige|Tōkaidōchū Hizakurige]]'', titled ''Miyajima Sankei Zoku Hizakurige''.<ref>{{Cite book|title=Miyajima Sankei Hizakurige|date=Unknown|publisher=Shunkōdō|page=16|url=https://www.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kotenseki/html/bunko06/bunko06_01003/index.html|accessdate=2021-12-07|location={{ill|Osaka|ja|大阪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439906}}|volume=Upper}}</ref>
== Monzen-machi == A {{ill|Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|lt=Monzen-machi|ja|門前町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monzenmachi|qq=}} (temple town) developed along the approach to the shrine and temple, catering to pilgrims. This area is also referred to as "Yugasan." Many stores operate only during peak visitation periods, such as New Year's.
The approach hosts eateries and souvenir shops, alongside a [[:en:Post office|post office]]. Traditional architecture remains, and conservation efforts are in place to maintain the historical streetscape.<ref name="kokuritsukouen" />
The area's specialty is '''{{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}}'''.
Nearby is {{ill|Yuga Onsen|ja|由加温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Historically, the {{ill|Tanokuchi Port|ja|田ノ口|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanokuchi Port|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the nearest port for pilgrims traveling to Yuga Shrine. The port town of Tanokuchi and adjacent {{ill|Karagoto|ja|唐琴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karagoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Shimonochō|ja|下の町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimonochō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} also prospered. Even today, a [[:en:Torii|torii]] gate remains in Tanokuchi. The name "Tanokuchi" is said to derive from "Yuga no Kuchi" (由加の口). == See Also == * {{ill|Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine|lt=Yuga Shrine Hongū|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Shrine Hongū|qq=}} * {{ill|Yugasan Rendaiji|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yugasan Rendaiji|qq=}} * {{ill|Kojima Peninsula|ja|児島半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Peninsula|1=Kojima Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388656}} * [[:en:Setonaikai National Park|Setonaikai National Park]] * {{ill|ankoro mochi|ja|あんころ餅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ankoro mochi|1=ankoro mochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11258809}} * {{ill|Kojima, Kurashiki|ja|児島地域 (倉敷市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima, Kurashiki|1=Kojima Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11388647}} * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira-gū]] * {{ill|Agura-ō|ja|阿久良王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agura-ō|1=Agura-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q48760699}}
